description: Game State Truth System — Make Prolog the ultimate arbiter of truth for all gameplay state. Define canonical predicates, add action prerequisites/effects, real-time truth sync, character templates, and creator tools.

userStories.title: Fix converter whitespace and clean Prolog output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clean Prolog output from the action converter with consistent formatting — no extra blank lines.
acceptanceCriteria: In shared/prolog/action-converter.ts, remove extra blank lines before '% Can Actor perform this action?' comment (single blank line only)
Remove trailing blank lines after prerequisite/effect blocks when they are empty
No double-blank-lines anywhere in generated output
Run migration to update all 133 base actions in DB with cleaned output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define canonical gameplay state predicates schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a definitive predicate schema for all gameplay-relevant state so that every system speaks the same Prolog vocabulary.
acceptanceCriteria: New file shared/prolog/gameplay-predicates.ts exports a GAMEPLAY_PREDICATES array

Each entry has: name (string), arity (number), description (string), args (array of {name, type} objects), syncDirection ('runtime_to_prolog' | 'prolog_to_runtime' | 'bidirectional'), category ('player_state' | 'npc_state' | 'world_state' | 'location')

Player state predicates defined: has_item/3, has_equipped/3, has_skill/3, has_trait/2, has_status/3, has_ability/2, health/3, energy/3, at_location/2, at_location_type/2, near/3, speaks_language/3, knows_vocabulary/3, gold/2, level/2, xp/3, age/2, occupation/2
NPC-specific predicates defined: npc_occupation/2, npc_schedule/4, npc_dialogue_state/2, npc_disposition/3, npc_will_trade/1, npc_quest_available/2
World state predicates defined: time_of_day/1, day_number/1, weather/1, location_accessible/1, location_discovered/1, quest_active/2, quest_completed/2, quest_failed/2
Location predicates defined: named_location/2, location_coords/3, location_of_building/2, location_of_business/2
Export getPredicatesByCategory(), getPredicateByName(), getMinimumViableTemplate() helper functions
getMinimumViableTemplate() returns the list of predicates required for any valid character: health, energy, speaks_language, gold, at_location, occupation, age
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define helper predicates for Prolog KB
userStories.description: As a developer, I need helper predicates (cefr_gte, is_weapon_type, etc.) in the base Prolog KB so that action prerequisites can reference them.
acceptanceCriteria: New file shared/prolog/helper-predicates.ts exports HELPER_PREDICATES_PROLOG string containing base Prolog helper rules
cefr_gte/2 predicate: compares CEFR levels (a1 < a2 < b1 < b2 < c1 < c2) — e.g. cefr_gte(b1, a2) succeeds
cefr_level_rank/2 maps atoms to integers: cefr_level_rank(a1, 1), cefr_level_rank(a2, 2), etc.
is_weapon_type/2: is_weapon_type(ItemId, Type) checks item_type(ItemId, Type) for weapon categories (sword, axe, bow, staff, pistol)
is_tool_type/2: checks item_type for tool categories (fishing_rod, pickaxe, axe, hoe)
skill_tier_name/2: maps integer skill levels to display names (1=novice, 3=apprentice, 5=journeyman, 7=expert, 10=master)
skill_gte/3: skill_gte(Actor, Skill, MinLevel) succeeds if has_skill(Actor, Skill, Level), Level >= MinLevel
GamePrologEngine loads these helpers at initialization
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define action prerequisites for combat actions
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want combat actions to require appropriate weapons and proximity so players can only fight when equipped.
acceptanceCriteria: New file shared/prolog/action-prerequisites.ts exports ACTION_PREREQUISITES: Record<string, PrerequisiteDefinition>
PrerequisiteDefinition type has: actionId (string), prerequisites (array of Prolog goal strings), inheritsFromParent (boolean)
attack_enemy: energy check + near(Actor, Target, 3) + alive(Target)
sword_attack, sword_combo, sword_dash: inherit from attack_enemy + has_equipped(Actor, weapon, W) + is_weapon_type(W, sword)
punch, punch_heavy, melee_hook: inherit from attack_enemy (no weapon required — unarmed)
shield_bash, shield_dash: inherit from attack_enemy + has_equipped(Actor, shield, _)
pistol_shoot: inherit from attack_enemy + has_equipped(Actor, weapon, W) + is_weapon_type(W, pistol)
throw_projectile: inherit from attack_enemy + has_item(Actor, projectile, _)
defend, shield_block: has_equipped(Actor, shield, _)
sword_block: has_equipped(Actor, weapon, W) + is_weapon_type(W, sword)
cast_spell, spell_channel: energy check + has_ability(Actor, magic)
Animation-only actions (react, die, hit_head, hit_reaction, knockback, sword_idle, pistol_aim, pistol_reload, zombie_*): inheritsFromParent = true, no additional prerequisites
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define action prerequisites for resource and commerce actions
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want resource gathering and commerce actions to require tools, location types, and merchant proximity.
acceptanceCriteria: In shared/prolog/action-prerequisites.ts, add prerequisites for resource category:
gather: energy check + at_location_type(Actor, LocType) where LocType is appropriate
chop_tree: inherit gather + has_item(Actor, axe, _) + at_location_type(Actor, forest)
mine_rock: inherit gather + has_item(Actor, pickaxe, _) + at_location_type(Actor, mine)
fish: inherit gather + has_item(Actor, fishing_rod, _) + at_location_type(Actor, water)
gather_herb: inherit gather + at_location_type(Actor, forest)
farm_plant, farm_water, farm_harvest: inherit farm + at_location_type(Actor, farm)
cook: energy check + at_location_type(Actor, kitchen)
craft_item, fix_repair: energy check + at_location_type(Actor, workshop)
Commerce prerequisites:
trade, buy_item, sell_item: near(Actor, Target, 5) + npc_will_trade(Target)
buy_item additionally: gold(Actor, G), G > 0
work: at_location(Actor, Workplace) + occupation(Actor, _)
paint, sweep, push_object: energy check only (inherit from work)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define action prerequisites for social, language, and remaining actions
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want social and language actions to require NPC proximity and CEFR levels respectively.
acceptanceCriteria: In shared/prolog/action-prerequisites.ts, add prerequisites for social category:
talk_to_npc and all children (greet, compliment_npc, insult_npc, threaten, flirt, persuade, bribe, gossip, confess, apologize, comfort, argue, joke, share_story, ask_about): near(Actor, Target, 5)
give_gift: near(Actor, Target, 5) + has_item(Actor, _, _) (must have something to give)
steal: near(Actor, Target, 3) + skill_gte(Actor, stealth, 1)
eavesdrop: near(Actor, Target, 15) (larger range)
request_quest: near(Actor, Target, 5) + npc_quest_available(Target, _)
Language prerequisites:
ask_for_directions, introduce_self: near(Actor, Target, 5) + speaks_language(Actor, _, _)
order_food: near(Actor, Target, 5) + npc_will_trade(Target) + speaks_language(Actor, Lang, Level) + cefr_gte(Level, a1)
haggle_price: near(Actor, Target, 5) + npc_will_trade(Target) + speaks_language(Actor, Lang, Level) + cefr_gte(Level, a2)
describe_scene: speaks_language(Actor, _, Level) + cefr_gte(Level, a2)
listen_and_repeat, answer_question, write_response: speaks_language(Actor, _, _)
teach_vocabulary: near(Actor, Target, 5) + speaks_language(Actor, Lang, Level) + cefr_gte(Level, b1)
read_book, read_sign: speaks_language(Actor, _, _)
Exploration: travel_to_location requires location_accessible(Loc), enter_building requires near + location_accessible
Items: equip_item, use_item, consume, drop_item, collect_item require has_item check
Survival: rest and sleep require energy check only; sit has no prerequisites
Movement/idle/expression: no prerequisites (animation-only)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define action effects for resource and commerce actions
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want resource and commerce actions to define their Prolog effects (item creation, gold transfer, XP, energy cost).
acceptanceCriteria: New file shared/prolog/action-effects.ts exports ACTION_EFFECTS: Record<string, EffectDefinition>
EffectDefinition type has: actionId (string), effects (array of Prolog effect term strings)
Resource effects: fish -> has_item(Actor, fish, 1), modify energy -15, skill XP fishing +1
chop_tree -> has_item(Actor, wood, 1), modify energy -15, skill XP woodcutting +1
mine_rock -> has_item(Actor, ore, 1), modify energy -20, skill XP mining +1
gather_herb -> has_item(Actor, herbs, 1), modify energy -10, skill XP herbalism +1
cook -> has_item(Actor, prepared_food, 1), modify energy -10, skill XP cooking +1
craft_item -> has_item(Actor, crafted_item, 1), modify energy -15, skill XP crafting +1
farm_plant/water/harvest -> appropriate item/energy/skill effects
Commerce effects: buy_item -> transfer_item + gold transfer, sell_item -> reverse
work -> modify gold(Actor, +wages), modify energy -20
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define action effects for combat, social, and language actions
userStories.description: As a game designer, I want combat, social, and language actions to define their Prolog effects.
acceptanceCriteria: In shared/prolog/action-effects.ts, add effects for combat category:
attack_enemy and children (sword_attack, punch, etc.): modify health(Target, -damage), modify energy(Actor, -cost), modify xp(Actor, +combat_xp)
defend and children: modify energy(Actor, -cost) only
Social effects:
talk_to_npc: assert(met(Actor, Target))
greet: modify(npc_disposition, Target, Actor, +5), assert(met(Actor, Target))
compliment_npc: modify(npc_disposition, Target, Actor, +10)
insult_npc: modify(npc_disposition, Target, Actor, -15)
give_gift: modify(npc_disposition, Target, Actor, +20), retract(has_item(Actor, Gift, _))
Language effects:
examine_object, point_and_name, read_sign: assert(knows_vocabulary(Actor, Lang, Category)), modify xp +5
listen_and_repeat: modify xp +10
write_response, answer_question: modify xp +15
All movement/idle/expression actions: no effects (animation-only)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update action converter to accept prerequisites and effects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the action converter to read from the prerequisite and effect definitions and generate proper Prolog can_perform rules and action_effect predicates.
acceptanceCriteria: Update convertActionToProlog() in shared/prolog/action-converter.ts to accept optional prerequisites: string[] and effects: string[] parameters
When prerequisites are provided, use them in can_perform rule body instead of just energy check
When effects are provided, emit action_effect(ActionId, Effect). for each effect
Parent action inheritance: if action has inheritsFromParent and a parentAction, include parent prerequisites in can_perform
Generated Prolog passes content validator with 0 errors
action_effect/2 warning in validator is resolved (effects are now present)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Migration: regenerate all 133 actions with prerequisites and effects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to re-run the action converter with the new prerequisite and effect definitions to update all base actions in MongoDB.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration server/migrations/040-action-prerequisites-effects.ts
Reads ACTION_PREREQUISITES and ACTION_EFFECTS for each action
Calls convertActionToProlog() with prerequisites and effects for each action
Updates content field on all 133 base actions in DB
All actions pass content validator with 0 errors
action_effect/2 warning eliminated for all player-triggerable actions
Migration is idempotent (can be re-run safely)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GameTruthSync class — equipment and inventory sync
userStories.description: As a developer, I need equipment and inventory changes to automatically assert/retract Prolog facts in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: New class GameTruthSync in shared/game-engine/logic/GameTruthSync.ts
Constructor takes GameEventBus and tau-prolog session references
Subscribes to item_equipped event: asserts has_equipped(player, Slot, ItemId), retracts previous equipped item in that slot
Subscribes to item_unequipped event: retracts has_equipped(player, Slot, ItemId)
Subscribes to item_collected event: asserts has_item(player, ItemName, Qty) or updates quantity
Subscribes to item_dropped event: retracts or decrements has_item(player, ItemName, Qty)
Subscribes to item_purchased event: asserts has_item for buyer, gold transfer
Subscribes to item_crafted event: asserts has_item(player, ItemName, Qty)
All assert/retract operations use local embedded tau-prolog (no API calls)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GameTruthSync — location, health, energy, and time sync
userStories.description: As a developer, I need player position, health, energy, and world time to sync to Prolog in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: GameTruthSync subscribes to location_visited/settlement_entered: retracts old at_location, asserts new at_location(player, LocationId)
Derives at_location_type from terrain/building data and asserts at_location_type(player, Type)
Subscribes to health change events: retracts old health/3, asserts health(player, NewCurrent, Max)
Tracks energy via action costs: retracts old energy/3, asserts energy(player, NewCurrent, Max)
Subscribes to hour_changed event: retracts old time_of_day/1, asserts time_of_day(NewHour)
Subscribes to day_changed event: retracts old day_number/1, asserts day_number(NewDay)
Subscribes to vocabulary_used event: asserts knows_vocabulary(player, Lang, Category) when new vocab learned
Subscribes to assessment_completed event: updates speaks_language(player, Lang, NewLevel)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GameTruthSync — save/load serialization
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Prolog fact base to be serialized with game saves and restored on load.
acceptanceCriteria: GameTruthSync has serialize() method that exports all asserted gameplay facts as a JSON-compatible array of {predicate, args} objects
GameTruthSync has restore(facts) method that asserts all facts into a fresh tau-prolog session
Serialize filters to only gameplay predicates (from GAMEPLAY_PREDICATES schema), not internal Prolog system predicates
Save integration: game save includes serialized fact base alongside existing JSON state
Load integration: on game load, restore facts before game loop starts
Cloud save includes serialized facts as backup
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NPC truth initialization from DB
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to have their truths initialized from character DB fields at game start.
acceptanceCriteria: GameTruthSync has initializeNpcTruths(characters) method
For each NPC character, asserts: person(NpcId), occupation(NpcId, Occ), at_location(NpcId, Loc), age(NpcId, Age)
Asserts has_skill(NpcId, Skill, Level) from character skills field
Asserts personality traits: personality(NpcId, Trait, Value) from Big Five
Asserts npc_occupation(NpcId, Occ) from occupation field
Asserts npc_will_trade(NpcId) for merchant-type NPCs
Asserts npc_quest_available(NpcId, QuestId) from available quests
Asserts health/3 and energy/3 with defaults for NPCs
Called during game initialization after world data loads
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NPC dynamic truth updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NPCs to dynamically gain/lose truths during gameplay as they move, trade, and change state.
acceptanceCriteria: GameTruthSync subscribes to npc_volition_action events: updates NPC at_location when NPCs move
Schedule system integration: when hour changes, updates NPC at_location and npc_schedule based on character schedules
Merchant stock: when NPC runs out of items, retracts npc_will_trade(NpcId) until restocked
NPC relationship changes: npc_relationship_changed event updates npc_disposition(NpcId, TowardId, Value)
NPC quest state: when quest given, retracts npc_quest_available for that quest
All NPC truth changes use same assert/retract pattern as player truths
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Character template schema and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database table for character templates that store starting truths.
acceptanceCriteria: Add character_templates table to shared/schema.ts with fields: id (UUID), worldId (FK nullable for base templates), name (text), description (text), startingTruths (jsonb array of {predicate: string, args: any[]}), isDefault (boolean), characterCreationMode referenced from worlds table
Add characterCreationMode field to worlds table: 'fixed' | 'archetype_select' | 'custom_create' (default 'fixed')
Add Mongoose schema for character_templates in server/db/mongo-storage.ts
Add CRUD methods: createCharacterTemplate, getCharacterTemplates, getCharacterTemplate, updateCharacterTemplate, deleteCharacterTemplate
Add API routes: GET/POST /api/worlds/:worldId/character-templates, GET/PUT/DELETE /api/character-templates/:id
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Seed default character templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need default character templates seeded for language-learning worlds.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration seeds 3 base character templates (worldId: null, isBase: true):

Explorer template: balanced stats, basic equipment (backpack, compass, map), skills (exploration 2, survival 1), speaks_language(english, b1) + speaks_language(french, a1), gold 100, health 100/100, energy 100/100

Scholar template: language-focused, notebook + quill + dictionary, skills (reading 3, writing 2, linguistics 1), speaks_language(english, b2) + speaks_language(french, a2), has_trait(bookworm), gold 50, health 80/80, energy 120/120
Trader template: commerce-focused, merchant_ledger + sample_goods, skills (bargaining 3, appraisal 2), speaks_language(english, b1) + speaks_language(french, a1), gold 200, health 90/90, energy 90/90
All templates include MVT predicates (health, energy, speaks_language, gold, at_location, occupation, age)
Migration is idempotent
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Character template editor UI — list and CRUD
userStories.description: As a world creator, I want to see, create, edit, and delete character templates in the editor.
acceptanceCriteria: New CharacterTemplateEditor component in client/src/components/ (or tab in existing world/character editor)
Lists all templates for the current world plus base templates
Create button opens form with name, description fields
Edit opens existing template
Duplicate creates a copy with '(Copy)' suffix
Delete with confirmation dialog
isDefault toggle (only one per world)
Reuses existing UI patterns (Card, Button, Dialog from shadcn)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Character template editor UI — predicate builder
userStories.description: As a world creator, I want a form-based predicate builder so I can add starting truths without writing Prolog.
acceptanceCriteria: Within template editor, 'Starting Truths' section with list of current predicates
Add Predicate button opens form: dropdown for predicate name (populated from GAMEPLAY_PREDICATES), typed inputs for each argument based on predicate arity and arg types
Predicate dropdown grouped by category (player_state, npc_state, world_state)
Preview panel shows the Prolog facts that would be asserted (formatted like: has_item(player, notebook, 1).)
Remove button on each predicate row
Validation: warns if MVT predicates are missing (health, energy, speaks_language, gold, at_location, occupation, age) with yellow banner
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Action feasibility tester
userStories.description: As a world creator, I want to test which actions a character template can perform so I can verify my game design.
acceptanceCriteria: New ActionFeasibilityTester component (panel within template editor or standalone)
Select a character template from dropdown
Displays all 133 actions grouped by category with pass/fail icons
Green checkmark = can_perform succeeds, red X = fails, gray dash = animation-only (no prerequisites)
Click a failed action to see which prerequisite(s) failed in a detail panel
'What's Missing?' helper: for failed actions, shows which predicates would need to be added to the template
Can modify template truths inline and click 'Re-test' without saving to DB
Uses local tau-prolog session initialized with template truths + helper predicates + action definitions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Integrate MVT context into LLM conversation prompts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Minimum Viable Template context to be included in NPC conversation prompts so the LLM knows the player's current game state.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit existing conversation prompt pipeline to identify where character/game state is currently injected
Add a generateMVTContext() function that queries the current Prolog fact base for MVT predicates and formats them as a natural-language context block
Context block includes: player health/energy, equipped items, inventory summary, gold, CEFR level, known vocabulary categories, current location, occupation
Context block is injected into NPC conversation system prompt alongside existing character context
Format is concise (under 200 tokens) to avoid bloating prompts
Typecheck passes
